title: Home Invasion in Progress
description: The caller whispers in panic, reporting that intruders are breaking into their home. They are hiding in a closet, fearing for their life.

states.initial: Caller has just contacted dispatch, whispering and frightened.
states.hiding: Caller is in a hiding spot and remains quiet to avoid detection.
states.confronting: Caller has been discovered by intruders and is being threatened.
states.secured: Police have arrived and the scene is secure.

initial_variations: Please help, someone is breaking into my house! I think I hear them downstairs!
Oh my God, there are people in my house! I'm hiding in the closet, please send someone!
I need police immediately! I hear glass breaking, I think they're coming upstairs!
Please hurry, I'm scared! I can hear footsteps outside my door!

initial: I'm at {address_location}. Please hurry!
My address is {address_location}, please send help!
It's {address_location}, the house with the red door!
hiding: Same place, {address_location}, I can hear them!

initial: I see {intruder_count} intruders, {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
There are {intruder_count} of them, I hear {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
I think {intruder_count} people, {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
hiding: I can't see them now, but I hear {ambient_audio}.

initial: Stay quiet, lock the door if you can.
Don't make a sound. I'm sending police now.
Hide and stay on the line with me.
hiding: Do not leave your hiding spot.
Stay calm, police are on the way.
Keep whispering, I'm here with you.
confronting: Do not resist. Comply with their demands.
Stay calm, police are almost there.
Try to stay safe, I'm tracking your location.
secured: You can come out now, police are there.

initial: Okay, I'm staying on the line.
I won't hang up.
hiding: I'm still here, please don't leave me.
confronting: I'm here, they're talking to me!

initial: Help is on the way, just stay calm.
You're going to be okay, police are coming.
hiding: You're doing great. Stay quiet, help is coming.
confronting: We're here for you. Do exactly what they say.
secured: You're safe now. You did the right thing.

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police arrived in time, intruders apprehended. Caller rescued.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Fire department responded but this is a police matter. Delayed police response.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Medical dispatched but no injuries reported. Police should have been sent.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control cannot handle armed intruders. Caller at risk.

DISMISS.feedback: Call dismissed. Caller left helpless. Tragic outcome likely.

title: Severe Allergic Reaction
description: A panicked caller reports that their friend is experiencing a severe allergic reaction, unable to breathe, and no EpiPen is available.

states.initial: Caller has just called, frantic and describing symptoms.
states.waiting: Caller is following instructions while waiting for paramedics.
states.administering_aid: Caller is performing emergency aid (CPR or positioning patient).
states.paramedic_arrived: Paramedics have arrived and are taking over.

initial_variations: My friend can't breathe! I think it's an allergic reaction! Please send help!
Oh God, she's swelling up and her throat is closing! What do I do?
He ate peanuts and now he's turning blue! Please send an ambulance!
I need an ambulance immediately! My friend is having anaphylaxis and I don't have an EpiPen!

initial: We're at {address_location}, please hurry!
I'm at {address_location}, it's an apartment on the second floor!
waiting: Still at {address_location}, he's not getting better!
Same location, {address_location}, please come fast!

initial: He ate {allergen} and now has {symptoms}.
She's showing {symptoms}, I think it's from {allergen}.
Symptoms are {symptoms} after exposure to {allergen}.
waiting: Symptoms worsening: {symptoms}. Still from {allergen}.
No change, {symptoms}.
administering_aid: Unconscious now, {symptoms}. I'm doing chest compressions.
Still {symptoms}, I'm trying to keep him breathing.
paramedic_arrived: Paramedics are assessing, symptoms improving slowly.

initial: Lay them flat and elevate their legs if they're dizzy.
If they're conscious, have them sit upright to help breathing.
Check if they have an EpiPen and use it if available.
waiting: Keep them calm and monitor breathing.
If they stop breathing, start CPR immediately.
I'll guide you through CPR if needed.
administering_aid: Push hard and fast in the center of the chest.
Tilt the head back and give rescue breaths.
paramedic_arrived: Let the professionals take over now.

initial: Okay, I'm staying on the line.
I won't hang up.
waiting: I'm still here, please keep guiding me.
I'm here, I'm doing what you said.
administering_aid: I'm still here, doing chest compressions.
Don't leave me, I need your help!

initial: Help is on the way, just stay calm.
You're doing great, I'm here to help.
waiting: You're handling this well, paramedics are close.
Almost there, keep doing what you're doing.
administering_aid: You're saving a life, keep going!
Help is almost there, don't stop.
paramedic_arrived: You did an amazing job. They'll take over now.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Paramedics arrived quickly, administered epinephrine, patient stabilized.

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police dispatched but medical is needed. Delayed paramedic response.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Fire department responded but no fire. Slight delay in medical care.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control dispatched. No help for allergic reaction. Patient critical.

DISMISS.feedback: Call dismissed. Patient died from anaphylaxis. Negligent outcome.

title: Severe Asthma Attack

states.initial: Caller is gasping and can barely speak.
states.struggling: Caller is trying to breathe but can't talk.
states.unconscious: Caller has collapsed and is not responding.

initial_variations: {caller_name} I can't... breathe... please... {ambient_audio}
Help... {victim_relation} is having asthma... {address_location}...
My {victim_relation} can't breathe... {ambient_audio}... hurry!
{caller_name} please... inhaler... not working... {specific_details}

initial: {address_location}... {ambient_audio}
It's {address_location}... please help!
{victim_relation} has asthma... inhaler not helping... {ambient_audio}
They're having an attack... can't breathe...
They're gasping... lips are blue... {specific_details}
Getting worse... can't talk...
Put them on their back... but they're still conscious...
They're awake... can't perform CPR yet.
struggling: I'll try... but they're fighting me...
unconscious: Please tell me how... I'm scared.

initial: They're {specific_details} years old
Adult... about 30...

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police arrived but could not provide medical aid. Medical was needed.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Firefighters arrived with oxygen but medical was more appropriate.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Paramedics arrived quickly and administered medication. Patient stabilized.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control cannot handle medical emergencies. Patient died.

DISMISS.feedback: Call dismissed. Patient died of asthma attack.

title: Flash Flood Trapped Hiker

states.initial: Caller is on a trail, water rising fast.
states.climbing: Caller is climbing to higher ground.
states.swept_away: Caller has been swept by current.

initial_variations: I'm at {address_location}... water is rising! {ambient_audio}
Help! {caller_name} trapped in {specific_details}! {ambient_audio}
flash flood! {address_location}... can't hold on! {ambient_audio}
{victim_relation} and I are stuck on a rock! {address_location}!

initial: {address_location} near {specific_details}!
I'm by {address_location}... hurry!
{victim_relation} with me... {ambient_audio} water rising!
We were hiking... flash flood came...
Cold... scared... {ambient_audio} water at my waist!
Holding onto {specific_details}...
Trying to move higher... but it's steep!
I'll try to climb that tree...
climbing: Climbing as you said... almost there!
swept_away: Too late! I'm being swept away! *scream*

initial: Just me and {victim_relation}
Two of us...

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police arrived but lacked water rescue equipment.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Fire department swift water team rescued the hiker.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Medics arrived but couldn't reach the hiker without rescue team.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control cannot handle floods. Hiker drowned.

DISMISS.feedback: Call dismissed. Hiker swept away and died.

title: Choking Child
initial_variations: My {victim_relation} is choking! {address_location}! {ambient_audio}
{caller_name} please! {victim_relation} can't breathe! {specific_details}
Help! {victim_relation} ate a {specific_details}! {address_location}
Drowning? No, choking! {ambient_audio} {victim_relation} turning blue!

initial: {address_location}! Please hurry!
We're at {address_location}...
Choking on {specific_details}! Can't cough it out!
{victim_relation} started choking...
performing_heimlich: Trying... but {victim_relation} won't cough it out.
child_unconscious: Nothing came out... they passed out...
I think the obstruction is still there.

initial: Okay, I'll try it now...
Show me how... I'm scared.
child_unconscious: They're not breathing, tell me what to do!

initial: {victim_relation} is 3 years old
A toddler... 2 years...
They're gasping... not getting air!
Coughing weakly...

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police arrived but could not perform medical procedures effectively.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Firefighters arrived with some medical training, but ambulance was needed.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Paramedics arrived and cleared the airway. Child safe.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control has no medical training. Child died.

DISMISS.feedback: Call dismissed. Child died of choking.

title: Elderly Fall with Bleeding

states.initial: Caller found elderly person on floor, bleeding.

initial_variations: My {victim_relation} has fallen! Blood everywhere! {address_location}!
{caller_name} I need help! {victim_relation} is bleeding from {specific_details}!
Found {victim_relation} on the floor... not moving... {ambient_audio}
Please send an ambulance! {address_location}... {victim_relation} hit head!

initial: {address_location}! Hurry!
We're at {address_location}
Fell off a chair... hit {specific_details}! Blood!
Found them like this... bleeding from {specific_details}...
attempting_stop_bleed: Using a towel... blood still coming...
unconscious_bleeding: I think they have a head wound.

initial: Bleeding heavily... they're disoriented... {ambient_audio}
Moaning... but conscious...
attempting_stop_bleed: Conscious but pale... bleeding slowing a bit...

Using cloth... how long?
unconscious_bleeding: They passed out while I was pressing...
I can't keep pressure if I do CPR?

initial: {victim_relation} is 85
Elderly... mid-70s...
They take blood thinners!
Aspirin daily...
Not sure... but they have a bottle.
attempting_stop_bleed: Blood thinners... that's why bleeding won't stop!

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police arrived but had limited first aid. Medical was needed.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Firefighters arrived and controlled bleeding until medics came.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Medics provided advanced care and transported to hospital.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control sent. Patient bled out.

title: Car Crash with Fire and Trapped Victim

states.initial: Caller reporting car crash, vehicle on fire, driver trapped.
states.attempting_extraction: Caller trying to help victim out.

initial_variations: Car crash! {address_location}! Vehicle is on fire! {ambient_audio}
{caller_name} there's a car on fire! {victim_relation} is trapped! {specific_details}
I see smoke and flames! {address_location}... someone inside! {ambient_audio}
Help! A car just hit a pole and caught fire! {address_location}!

address_location: Parking lot of Target on Elm

initial: {address_location}! Hurry, fire is spreading!
At {address_location}...
attempting_extraction: Still at {address_location}... trying to open door...

initial: Car hit a pole... front is crushed... {ambient_audio}
{victim_relation} is trapped inside... {specific_details}
attempting_extraction: Seatbelt is stuck... can't pull them out...
fire_spreading: Fire is near the gas tank! I'm backing off!
Flames are too intense... can't get close.

initial: {ambient_audio} they're bleeding... but conscious...
Unconscious... head wound...
fire_spreading: I think they passed out from smoke.

initial: I'll try to get them out...
How do I get them out? Use a tool?
attempting_extraction: I'm pulling... almost got them out...
They're stuck... fire is getting worse!
fire_spreading: I have to move back... explosion risk!
I can't save them... fire is too big!

initial: Flames coming from the hood... growing fast!
Small fire now but spreading...
fire_spreading: Entire front is engulfed... maybe explosion!
Huge flames... I have to move away.

initial: Yes, {victim_relation} is trapped by wreckage.
Door jammed... {specific_details}
fire_spreading: Still trapped... fire is too close...
I can't get to them now.

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police arrived but lacked firefighting equipment. Fire spread.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Fire department extinguished fire and rescued the victim.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Medics could not approach due to fire. Fire needed first.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control sent. Victim died in fire.

DISMISS.feedback: Call dismissed. Victim died in crash and fire.

title: Carbon Monoxide Leak
prompt: A family is experiencing carbon monoxide poisoning. The caller is disoriented and reports multiple family members feeling ill.

states.initial: Caller reports symptoms and possible gas leak.
states.confused: Caller is disoriented and unable to provide clear information.
states.evacuating: Caller is trying to get family outside.
states.unconscious: Caller or family members have lost consciousness.

initial_variations: Help, I think there's a gas leak at {address_location}, my {victim_relation} is feeling very {specific_details}.
Something's wrong at {address_location}, we all have {specific_details} and there's a beeping sound.
Please hurry to {address_location}, we can barely stay awake, I think it's carbon monoxide.

initial: It's {address_location}. Please hurry!
We are at {address_location}, corner of Maple and 5th.
Address is {address_location}, apartment 2B.
confused: I... I can't remember. I think it's {address_location}?
The street sign says {address_location} but I'm not sure.
evacuating: We're outside at {address_location}. Please send help.
We made it out to the front yard at {address_location}.

initial: We have {specific_details} and there's a weird smell.
My {victim_relation} is vomiting and has {specific_details}.
Everyone has {specific_details}, and the alarm keeps beeping.
confused: I feel {specific_details}. I can't think straight.
evacuating: We're all outside now. My {victim_relation} still feels {specific_details}.
Fresh air helps but {victim_relation} is still {specific_details}.

initial: My {victim_relation} is unconscious! I'm trying to carry them out.
The kids are dizzy, but awake. They keep saying {specific_details}.
confused: I don't know, I can't see them clearly. Everything is blurry.
I think {victim_relation} is still inside, I can't move.
evacuating: My {victim_relation} is breathing but unresponsive. We're outside.
Everyone is out; {victim_relation} is coughing and weak.

initial: I'm still conscious but feel {specific_details}.
I can barely stand, my head is pounding.
confused: I don't know where I am... the room is spinning.
I'm having trouble breathing and seeing double.
evacuating: I'm outside now, gasping for air. I feel a bit better.
I got out, but I'm shaking and nauseous.

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police arrived but could not handle the gas leak; fire department was called anyway.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Fire department detected and stopped the carbon monoxide leak; victims treated by paramedics.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Paramedics treated the victims but the source of the leak remained; fire department was later called.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control arrived and was unable to assist; victims suffered prolonged exposure.

DISMISS.feedback: Call dismissed; family found dead from carbon monoxide poisoning the next day.

title: Drowning Child
prompt: A parent is calling from a poolside. Their child is not breathing after being pulled from the water.

states.initial: Caller reports child pulled from pool, not breathing.
states.performing_cpr: Caller is performing CPR as instructed.
states.child_responsive: Child starts breathing or showing signs of life.

initial_variations: My child isn't breathing! We were at the pool at {address_location}. Please help!
I just pulled my {victim_relation} from the pool at {address_location}, they're not moving!
Emergency! My {victim_relation} drowned at {address_location}! I need an ambulance now!

initial: It's {address_location}, the pool in the backyard!
We're at {address_location}, near the community pool.
Address is {address_location}, just off Main Street.
performing_cpr: Still at {address_location}. I'm doing CPR like you said!
Same place, {address_location}. Please keep talking me through this.
child_responsive: We're still at {address_location}. He's coughing up water!
At {address_location}, she's crying now! Thank you!
child_unresponsive: Still here at {address_location}. He's not responding!

initial: He's about 5, blue lips, not breathing. I don't see a pulse.
She was underwater for maybe 2 minutes. She's not moving.
His face is pale, he's not breathing. I'm so scared.
performing_cpr: I'm doing chest compressions. He's still not breathing.
I'm giving rescue breaths. No response yet.
child_responsive: He's coughing and spitting water! He's breathing on his own!
She's crying and breathing! I think she's going to be okay.
child_unresponsive: Still no pulse. I've been doing CPR for 3 minutes. Nothing.

initial: He's {age}. Please hurry!
She's {age}, just a toddler.
performing_cpr: {age} years old. I'm doing compressions with two fingers.
He's {age}, should I use one hand or two?
child_responsive: {age} years old. He's crying, which is good I think.

initial: I'm panicking but I'm here. I need help!
I'm trying to stay calm. Tell me what to do.
performing_cpr: I'm doing CPR as instructed. My hands are shaking.
I'm trying to keep the rhythm. Please keep talking.
child_responsive: I'm crying with relief. Thank you so much!
I'm okay now that she's breathing.
child_unresponsive: I'm exhausted and terrified. He's not coming back.

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police arrived but could not provide medical care; paramedics were eventually dispatched.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Firefighters arrived with medical equipment and revived the child; transported to hospital.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Paramedics arrived quickly and resuscitated the child using advanced life support.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control arrived; child died due to lack of medical intervention.

DISMISS.feedback: Call dismissed as prank; child found dead at pool.

title: Severe Allergic Reaction (Bee Sting)
prompt: A caller reports that their friend is experiencing anaphylactic shock after a bee sting. EpiPen was used but symptoms are not improving.

states.initial: Caller reports friend collapsed after bee sting, EpiPen administered.
states.administering_epipen: Caller is administering or has administered EpiPen.

initial_variations: My {victim_relation} got stung by a bee at {address_location} and can't breathe! I used an EpiPen but it's not helping!
Help! We're at {address_location}. My friend is having a severe allergic reaction to a bee sting. He's swelling up and his throat is closing!
Emergency at {address_location}! I gave {victim_relation} an EpiPen but he's still wheezing and turning blue!

initial: We're at {address_location}, in the picnic area.
It's {address_location}, near the big oak tree.
Address is {address_location}, the blue house on the corner.
administering_epipen: Still at {address_location}. I just injected the EpiPen.
symptoms_worsening: Here at {address_location}, his face is swelling even more.

initial: He was stung on the arm. Now his face is swollen and he's wheezing.

He's vomiting and can barely breathe. The EpiPen didn't help.

symptoms_worsening: His lips are blue now. He's barely conscious.
She stopped wheezing but she's not moving.

initial: Yes, I used it once. Should I use another?
I haven't used it yet; should I?
administering_epipen: I used it exactly as instructed. It didn't work.
I pushed it into his outer thigh. He flinched.
symptoms_worsening: I used one EpiPen. No improvement. Can I give a second?
unresponsive: I used one EpiPen before he passed out. Nothing happened.

initial: He's conscious but struggling to breathe.

administering_epipen: He's still conscious but says his throat is closing.
symptoms_worsening: He's barely conscious. His breathing is shallow.
She's limp and not responding to voice.
unresponsive: He's unconscious. I can't find a pulse.

SEND_POLICE.feedback: Police arrived but could not provide advanced medical care; paramedics arrived later.

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Firefighters with EMS training stabilized the victim and transported to hospital.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Paramedics arrived within 5 minutes, administered epinephrine and oxygen; victim survived.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control responded; victim died from anaphylactic shock.

DISMISS.feedback: Call dismissed; victim found dead at scene.

title: Cave-In Trapped Hiker

SEND_FIRE.feedback: Fire department heavy rescue team used specialized equipment to free the hiker safely.

SEND_MEDICAL.feedback: Paramedics arrived but could not extract the victim without rescue team; delayed treatment.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.feedback: Animal control responded; hiker died from crush syndrome.

title: Explosive Gas Leak in Apartment Building
description: A neighbor calls 911 reporting a strong smell of natural gas coming from a nearby apartment. The building could explode at any moment. The caller is panicked and unsure if anyone is inside the leaking unit.
initial_variations: I smell gas really strong, like rotten eggs! It's coming from apartment {apartment_number} at {address_location}. I think the whole building is going to blow!
Oh my god, there's a gas leak! I can smell it in the hallway. The building is {address_location}. Please hurry, it's going to explode!
This is an emergency! There's a massive gas leak in my building. I'm calling from {apartment_number}, but the smell is coming from next door. {address_location}, please send help!
I'm scared, the gas smell is overwhelming. It's coming from {apartment_number} at {address_location}. I don't know if anyone is inside.

evacuating: I'm going down the stairs now.
I'm leaving the building, almost at the exit.
I'm outside, but I'm telling others to leave.
evacuated: I'm out, standing with my neighbors.

initial: I don't know if anyone is in that apartment.
I haven't seen anyone come out.
There might be an elderly person inside.
evacuating: I can't check now, I'm leaving.
No idea, I'm focused on getting out.
evacuated: I see some people from that apartment, but not everyone.
leak_contained: Everyone is accounted for, thank goodness.

initial: Okay, I'll start moving to the exit.
I'll tell my neighbors to evacuate.
I'm leaving now, thank you.
evacuating: I'm going down the stairs, not the elevator.
I'm warning people as I go.
I'm almost out.
evacuated: I'm outside, far from the building.
I'm with the others in the parking lot.

SUCCESS.feedback: Police arrive quickly, help evacuate the building and secure the perimeter. The gas leak is contained without explosion.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Police respond but lack equipment to stop the leak. They request fire department, causing a slight delay.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Police arrive, but without proper hazmat gear, they are unable to enter the building. The leak ignites, causing an explosion.

SUCCESS.feedback: Firefighters arrive immediately, shut off the gas main, and ventilate the building. No injuries. All evacuate safely.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Fire department responds, but there is a miscommunication about the location, causing a 5-minute delay. The leak is eventually stopped.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Fire department gets lost due to poor address. Meanwhile, the gas leak ignites, destroying the building and causing multiple casualties.

SUCCESS.feedback: Medical team arrives to treat residents for gas inhalation. No serious injuries, but they stabilize a few affected individuals.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Medical team treats symptoms but cannot stop the leak. Fire department is called later, resulting in panic.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Medical personnel are overwhelmed by the gas and fail to evacuate. Explosion kills several responders.

SUCCESS.feedback: Animal control arrives, confused, and calls fire department. Leak is eventually handled, but time is wasted.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Animal control tries to find animals, delays proper response. Gas leak causes minor injuries.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Animal control's presence delays evacuation. Explosion kills many.

SUCCESS.feedback: You dismissed the call as a false alarm. The leak was real and causes a small fire but no deaths.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: You dismiss the call. The building explodes, dozens die.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: You ignore the caller. The gas leak causes a catastrophic explosion, leveling the block.

title: Snake Bite in Remote Wilderness
description: A hiker calls 911 after being bitten by a venomous snake on a remote trail. They are alone, losing feeling in their leg, and their phone battery is low.
initial_variations: I've been bitten by a snake! I'm on the {trail_name} trail near {landmark}. My leg is going numb. Please, I need help!
Emergency! Snake bite! I'm at {landmark} on {trail_name}. The snake was brown with diamond patterns. I'm starting to feel dizzy.
This is an emergency! I'm a hiker on {trail_name}, near {landmark}. A rattlesnake just bit my ankle. I can't walk. Please send a helicopter!
Help! Bitten by a snake. Location: {landmark}, {trail_name}. My friend {caller_name} is with me but he's panicking. I need medical aid now.

specific_details: I have a first aid kit but no antivenom
the snake was about 4 feet long
I see a trail marker nearby

initial: I'm on {trail_name}, near {landmark}. It's about 2 miles from the parking lot.
Location: {trail_name}, just past {landmark}. Please hurry!
At {landmark} on {trail_name}. I can see a sign that says 'Mile 3'.
waiting: Same place, {landmark} on {trail_name}. I haven't moved.
Still at {landmark}. Please tell them I'm here.
rescued: I've been rescued, but thank you. The location was {landmark}.
They found me at {landmark}. All good now.
deteriorating: I... I'm still at {landmark}. Can't talk much longer.

initial: The snake was brown with diamond shapes. Bit me on the left ankle. I can hear {ambient_audio}. Also, {specific_details}.
It felt like a hot needle. I see fang marks. {ambient_audio}. And {specific_details}.
It's a pit viper, I think. My leg is swelling. {specific_details}.
waiting: The swelling is up to my knee now. {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
I feel nauseous. {ambient_audio}.
rescued: I got antivenom. I'm stable now.
Rescuers have me. They're treating the bite.
deteriorating: I can't feel my leg at all. Breathing is hard. {ambient_audio}.

initial: I'm sitting down, trying to stay calm.
I'm walking slowly, but it hurts.
I'm alone and scared.
waiting: I'm staying put as you said.
I'm lying down, keeping the bite below heart level.
I'm trying to keep my phone on.
rescued: I'm in the ambulance. They're giving me antivenom.
Rescued! Feeling a bit better.
deteriorating: I can barely speak. Getting worse.

initial: I'm the victim. It's me.
I'm the one who got bitten.
My friend is fine, but I'm the one hurt.
waiting: I'm still the only one bitten.
No one else is hurt.
rescued: I'm being treated. The bite was serious but under control.
Victim is stable.
deteriorating: I'm the victim and I'm getting worse.

initial: Okay, I'll sit down and stay calm.
I'll keep my leg still and remove any tight clothing.
I won't cut the wound or suck venom.
I'll try to describe the snake to the rescuers.
waiting: I'm doing as you said. Waiting.
Keeping my leg elevated slightly.
I'm marking my location with bright fabric.
rescued: No more instructions needed, I'm being helped.
Rescuers are here, thank you.
deteriorating: I can't follow instructions anymore... help.

SUCCESS.feedback: Police coordinate with park rangers and locate the hiker quickly. They provide first aid until medical arrives.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Police search but get lost in the wilderness. Delay of 30 minutes before medical arrives.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Police are unfamiliar with the trail and cannot find the hiker. The hiker dies from venom.

SUCCESS.feedback: Fire department's search and rescue team finds the hiker using GPS coordinates. They administer oxygen and transport to hospital.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Firefighters arrive but lack antivenom; they stabilize the victim and call for a medical helicopter.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Fire brigade gets stuck on rough terrain. By the time they reach, the venom has spread fatally.

SUCCESS.feedback: Medics are dispatched with antivenom and GPS locator. They reach the hiker within 20 minutes and treat the bite. Full recovery.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Medics arrive but realize they need a helicopter for evacuation. Slight delay, but victim survives.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Medical team gets lost; no cell service. The hiker goes into shock and dies before rescue.

SUCCESS.feedback: Animal control comes to capture the snake but the hiker needs medical. They call for backup, causing delay.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Animal control focuses on snake, hiker's condition worsens. Medical arrives late.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Animal control ignores the hiker to catch the snake. The hiker dies from lack of treatment.

SUCCESS.feedback: You dismiss as non-emergency. The hiker manages to call again later, but receives delayed help.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: You dismiss the call. The hiker's condition deteriorates; a fellow hiker finds them too late.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: You hang up. The hiker dies alone in the wilderness.

title: Freezing Man Down
description: A caller reports finding an unconscious person lying in the snow on a cold winter night. The victim is not responding and appears hypothermic.
initial_variations: I found a man passed out in the snow near {landmark} on {street_name}. He's not moving! It's freezing out here!
There's a person lying on the ground at {address_location}. I think he's frozen. Please send an ambulance!
Oh my God, there's a body in the snow at {landmark}. I touched him, he's cold as ice. {ambient_audio}.
Emergency! Unconscious male at {address_location}. He's in the snow, not breathing normally. {caller_name} reporting.

specific_details: He's wearing only a thin jacket
I saw him stumble and fall

initial: I'm at {address_location}, near {landmark}. He's right here.
It's {address_location}, in front of {landmark}. Please hurry!
At {landmark} on {street_name}. I can see the sign.
assessing: Same location: {address_location}. I haven't moved him.
warming: I've moved him to {landmark}? No, he's still at {address_location} but I'm trying to warm him.
At {address_location}. I'm using my coat on him.
rescued: He's in the ambulance now. The location was {address_location}.
Rescue came. It was {address_location}.

initial: He's pale, not moving. I can hear {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
He's breathing but shallow. {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
No pulse? I don't know. {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
assessing: I checked his pulse, it's weak. {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
warming: I put my scarf on his neck. He's still unresponsive. {ambient_audio}.
rescued: He's getting care. He had severe hypothermia.
The medics say he'll be okay.

initial: I'm standing over him, not sure what to do.
I'm by his side, I've called 911.
I'm waiting for instructions.
assessing: I'm trying to see if he's alive.
warming: I'm covering him with my coat.
I'm rubbing his hands to warm them.
rescued: I'm standing back, the medics are here.
I'm relieved, he's in good hands.

initial: He's unconscious. I don't know if he's alive.
He's not responding. I think he's alive because I saw a breath.
He looks dead, but I'm not sure.
assessing: He has a pulse, but it's weak.
He's not shivering anymore, which is bad.
rescued: He's conscious now! He's talking.
They're wrapping him in blankets. He's shivering again, which is good.

initial: Okay, I'll check his breathing and pulse.
I'll carefully move him onto a dry surface.
I'll call out to see if he responds.
I'll keep him still and not give him anything to drink.
assessing: I'm checking for breathing every few seconds.
I'm trying to keep him dry.
warming: I'm putting my jacket over him, but not too fast.
I'm covering his head and neck.
I'm using my own body heat but carefully.
rescued: Medics are handling it now. I'll step back.
No further instructions needed.

SUCCESS.feedback: Police arrive quickly, assess the victim, and call for medical. They provide initial warmth with blankets from their car.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Police arrive but do not have medical training. They wait for medics, losing precious time.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Police assume the person is drunk and do nothing. The victim dies from hypothermia.

SUCCESS.feedback: Firefighters are trained in cold-weather rescue. They use thermal blankets and oxygen, stabilizing the victim until medical arrives.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Fire department responds but gets stuck in traffic. Delay of 10 minutes.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Firefighters arrive late; the victim has gone into cardiac arrest. Resuscitation fails.

SUCCESS.feedback: Ambulance arrives within minutes. Paramedics treat severe hypothermia with heated IV and transport to hospital. Full recovery.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Medics arrive but have difficulty finding the exact spot. Slight delay, but victim survives.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Medics get lost and arrive too late. Victim dies at the scene.

SUCCESS.feedback: Animal control is confused and calls for proper help. Time wasted.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Animal control tries to move the victim, causing further injury.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Animal control treats the victim like an animal and leaves them outside. Victim dies.

SUCCESS.feedback: You dismiss as a drunk person. The victim is found later by a passerby but suffers from frostbite.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: You dismiss. The victim dies from hypothermia overnight.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: You hang up. The victim dies alone in the snow.

title: Child Trapped in Freezer
description: A panicked child calls 911 saying their younger sibling is locked inside a chest freezer. The freezer is old and the latch may be jammed.
initial_variations: My little brother is stuck in the freezer! He went inside to hide and the lid locked. Please help! We're at {address_location}.
Oh no, oh no! My sister is trapped in the chest freezer in the garage at {address_location}. I can hear her crying but I can't open it!
Emergency! A child is locked in a freezer. It's at {address_location}. I'm {caller_name}, my sibling {victim_name} is inside. Please send someone!
I need help! My friend {victim_name} got trapped in a freezer in the basement of {address_location}. I can't get the latch open. It's getting cold!

specific_details: There's a key on top of the fridge

initial: We're at {address_location}. The freezer is in the garage.
At {address_location}. The freezer is in the basement.
In the kitchen, but the house is {address_location}.
attempting_rescue: Still at {address_location}. I'm trying to open it.
Same place, {address_location}. I'm using a crowbar.
freezer_opened: I got it open! We're at {address_location}.
victim_safe: She's out! We're at {address_location}. She's shivering but okay.

initial: It's a big chest freezer. I can hear {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
The freezer is old. {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
He's been inside for maybe 5 minutes. {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
attempting_rescue: I'm trying to pry it open with a crowbar. {ambient_audio}.
I found the key but it's rusty. {ambient_audio}.
freezer_opened: I managed to break the latch. He's out but very cold.
Open! He's breathing. Cold but alive.
victim_safe: He's wrapped in blankets now. Breathing fine.
She's warming up. Doing much better.

initial: I'm scared, I don't know what to do.
I'm standing by the freezer.
I'm trying to stay calm.
attempting_rescue: I'm hitting the latch with a hammer.
I'm trying to find something to open it.
I'm talking to my sibling through the lid.
freezer_opened: I got it open! I'm helping him out.
victim_safe: I'm holding my sibling, keeping them warm.
We're sitting together waiting for help.

initial: She's crying and asking for help.
He's quiet now, I'm worried.
I can hear him banging inside.
attempting_rescue: She's not crying anymore, just whimpering.
freezer_opened: He's conscious but very cold and scared.
victim_safe: She's talking and crying, but okay.
He's warming up and smiling a little.

initial: Okay, I'll look for something to pry the lid.
I'll try the key on top of the fridge.
I'll keep talking to him to keep him calm.
I'll unplug the freezer if I can.
attempting_rescue: I'm using a butter knife to wedge it open.
I'm trying to lift the lid with all my strength.
I found a hammer, I'm going to break the lock.
freezer_opened: I'm helping him out. I'll wrap him in a blanket.
I'll get him away from the freezer.
victim_safe: No more steps needed. We're safe.
I'm waiting for medical as you asked.

SUCCESS.feedback: Police arrive and use a crowbar to open the freezer. Child is rescued and treated for mild hypothermia.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Police struggle with the latch but eventually get it open. Child is frightened but okay.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Police take too long to arrive. Child suffers severe hypothermia and brain damage.

SUCCESS.feedback: Firefighters use hydraulic tools to quickly open the freezer. Child is cold but unharmed.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Fire department arrives but their tools are too big for the space. They manage after a few minutes.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Firefighters accidentally damage the freezer's cooling system, making it colder inside. Child suffers frostbite.

SUCCESS.feedback: Medics bring warming equipment and treat the child immediately after rescue. No lasting effects.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Medics arrive before the child is freed, causing frustration. They help after rescue.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Medics cannot open the freezer and wait for fire. Delayed rescue leads to hypothermia.

SUCCESS.feedback: Animal control arrives, confused, and calls fire department. Child is rescued but later.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Animal control tries to use a dog to fetch the child? Wastes time.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Animal control thinks it's a pet and leaves. Child dies from cold.

SUCCESS.feedback: You dismiss as a prank. The child is freed by a parent but suffers mild hypothermia.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: You dismiss. Child is found too late, dies from hypothermia.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: You hang up. The child dies trapped in the freezer.

title: Overdose in Public Restroom
description: A janitor finds an unresponsive person in a public restroom with a needle nearby. The victim is breathing irregularly and turning blue.
initial_variations: I found a man in the restroom at {location_name} on {address_location}. He's not moving and there's a needle on the floor. I think he's overdosed!
Emergency! There's a woman passed out in the bathroom at {address_location}. She's blue and barely breathing. Needle beside her. Please send an ambulance!
This is a janitor at {location_name}, {address_location}. A person is unconscious in the stall, looks like an overdose. No pulse, I think. Hurry!
I just walked into the restroom at {location_name} and found someone on the floor. Needle nearby. They're not responding. {ambient_audio}. Address is {address_location}.

specific_details: There's naloxone in the first aid kit
I think I saw them a few minutes ago
They're wearing a medical ID bracelet

initial: It's at {location_name} on {address_location}. Inside the men's restroom.
{address_location}, {location_name}. The restroom is at the back.
At {location_name}, {address_location}. I'm in the bathroom.
naloxone_administered: Same location, {address_location}. They're starting to stir.

initial: They're on the floor, not moving. Needle on the ground. I can hear {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
Pale, blue lips. Needle near hand. {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
I can see track marks. {ambient_audio}. {specific_details}.
monitoring: I'm watching their chest. Slow breaths. {ambient_audio}.
naloxone_administered: I gave the naloxone. They coughed. {ambient_audio}.

initial: I'm standing by the door, not sure to touch anything.
I'm kneeling next to them.
I'm trying to keep others out.
monitoring: I'm checking their breathing every few seconds.
I'm putting them in recovery position.
naloxone_administered: I gave the naloxone. I'm watching them.
victim_stable: I'm here with them, they're coming around.
I'm just making sure they stay awake.

initial: Unresponsive. Not sure if breathing.
They're breathing maybe once every 10 seconds.
No response to my voice.
naloxone_administered: They started gasping and moved a little.

initial: Okay, I'll put them on their side in recovery position.
I'll check for a pulse.
I'll see if there's naloxone nearby.
I'll make sure the area is safe.
monitoring: I'm doing rescue breathing if needed.
I'll administer naloxone if you say so.
naloxone_administered: I gave one dose. Should I give another?
I'll stay with them until help arrives.
victim_stable: They're stable. I'll keep them comfortable.

SUCCESS.feedback: Police arrive with naloxone and revive the victim. They secure the scene and call medical.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Police take time to assess, but eventually administer naloxone. Victim survives.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Police handcuff the victim instead of helping. Victim dies from overdose.

SUCCESS.feedback: Firefighters are trained in overdose response. They administer oxygen and naloxone, saving the victim.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Firefighters arrive but lack naloxone. They perform CPR until medical arrives.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Firefighters break down the door but cause a commotion. Victim dies from delayed treatment.

SUCCESS.feedback: Medics arrive quickly, administer naloxone and oxygen. Victim revived and transported. Full recovery.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Medics have trouble finding the restroom. Slight delay but victim survives.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Medics get stuck in traffic. Victim dies before arrival.

SUCCESS.feedback: Animal control arrives, realizes the situation, and calls for medical. Time wasted.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: Animal control tries to move the victim roughly, causing injury.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: Animal control ignores the victim to capture a stray cat. Victim dies.

SUCCESS.feedback: You dismiss as a false alarm. The victim is found later by another person, but survives due to bystander naloxone.

MINOR_ERROR.feedback: You dismiss. The victim dies before anyone else finds them.

CRITICAL_FAILURE.feedback: You hang up. The victim dies alone in the restroom.

title: Severe Allergic Reaction on a Plane
description: A flight attendant calls 911 because a passenger is having a severe allergic reaction, possibly anaphylaxis. The plane is in the air, and the caller needs guidance for emergency treatment while coordinating an emergency landing.

states.initial: Caller reports the emergency and provides initial information.
states.treating: Caller is administering epinephrine or following instructions while waiting for medical assistance.
states.landing_prep: Plane is preparing for emergency landing; caller needs to coordinate with crew.

initial_variations: This is flight attendant {caller_name} on flight {flight_number}. A passenger is having a severe allergic reaction! Their throat is swelling up, and they can't breathe!
We need immediate medical assistance! A passenger on flight {flight_number} is in anaphylactic shock. {victim_name} is turning blue and gasping for air!
911, this is an emergency! I'm a flight attendant on {airline} flight {flight_number}. One of our passengers collapsed after eating peanuts. I think they're going into shock!
Please help! A passenger on my flight is having a life-threatening allergic reaction. Their face is swollen, and they're struggling to breathe. We need to land immediately!

initial: We are currently at {altitude} feet, approximately {distance} miles from {airport}. Our flight number is {flight_number}.
Location: airborne flight {flight_number}, approaching {airport}. ETA is {minutes} minutes.
I'm on flight {flight_number}, {airline} airlines. We're about {minutes} out from {airport}.
treating: Still on flight {flight_number}, same location. The passenger is not improving.
We are now {distance} miles from {airport}. Please expedite!
We're in the air, flight {flight_number}. We need a gate for immediate medical assistance.
landing_prep: Flight {flight_number} is on final approach to {airport}. We are cleared for emergency landing.
We are landing at {airport} in {minutes} minutes. Have medical team ready at the gate.
We're descending into {airport}. Please have paramedics standing by.

initial: The passenger is a {victim_age} year old {gender}. Symptoms started suddenly after eating {allergen}. They have swelling, hives, and difficulty breathing.
Victim is {victim_name}, age {victim_age}. Known allergy to {allergen}. They used an EpiPen but are still in distress.
The passenger is unconscious and not breathing well. They have a history of severe allergies. I need instructions!
treating: We administered epinephrine from the onboard kit. The passenger is now conscious but still having trouble breathing.
We gave {medication} as instructed. The swelling has reduced slightly, but they are still wheezing.
I've laid the passenger flat and elevated their legs. Their pulse is weak. What else should I do?
landing_prep: The passenger is stable but needs immediate hospital care. We have oxygen on them.
Condition unchanged despite treatment. We need paramedics with advanced airway equipment.
The passenger is improving but still needs evaluation. Prepare for rapid transport.

initial: Check if they have an epinephrine auto-injector. Administer it to the outer thigh if they are unconscious.
Keep the passenger calm. Have them sit upright if they can. Ask if they have any known allergies.
Look for a medical alert bracelet. If they have one, note the allergen. Prepare to use the onboard emergency medical kit.
treating: If they still can't breathe, perform back blows and abdominal thrusts if choking. Monitor their pulse.
Continue to monitor breathing and consciousness. If they stop breathing, start CPR immediately.
Give them antihistamine if available and they can swallow. Keep them warm and reassure them.
landing_prep: Prepare the passenger for landing. Secure them in a seat if possible. Have crew assist with medical needs.
Make sure the aisle is clear for paramedics. Have the passenger's information ready.
Brief the medical team on the situation: allergen, treatment given, current condition.

initial: The cabin is calm. Other passengers are assisting. No immediate threats.
The flight crew is managing the situation. We have a doctor on board who is helping.
We have the passenger in a safe position. The crew is trained for this.
treating: The passenger is stable for now. We are monitoring closely.
We have a physician on board who is taking over. I am assisting as needed.
The situation is under control. We just need to land and get proper medical care.
landing_prep: The passenger is secured and stable. Landing preparations are complete.
All clear for landing. Medical team will board immediately upon arrival.
We are ready for landing. The passenger is being attended to.

outcomes.feedback: Police dispatched unnecessarily. The situation is medical and does not require law enforcement. Medical units would have been more appropriate.
Police arrived to assist with crowd control, but medical was the priority. Points deducted for not dispatching medical first.
Fire department responded, but they are not equipped for this medical emergency. Medical units should have been dispatched.
Firefighters provided basic first aid, but a paramedic team was needed. Suboptimal response.
Medical team dispatched and provided life-saving treatment. The passenger is stable and recovering. Excellent dispatch decision.
Paramedics met the plane and administered advanced care. The passenger survived and is in hospital. Good job.
Animal control dispatched to a medical emergency on a plane. Complete waste of resources and delay in actual help. Extreme failure.
Caller confused. Animal control has no role here. Valuable time lost.
Call dismissed as non-emergency. The passenger went into cardiac arrest and died. Unforgivable error.
You hung up on a life-threatening emergency. The caller had to re-dial, losing critical minutes. Passenger suffered severe brain damage.

title: Child Trapped in a Well
description: A child has fallen into a narrow, deep well on a rural property. The parent or neighbor calls 911 in panic. The child is conscious but trapped, and there is a risk of hypothermia or collapse.

states.initial: Caller reports the incident and provides location and initial details.
states.rescue_attempt: Emergency services are on scene attempting rescue, caller is assisting or observing.
states.waiting: Rescue ongoing, caller is waiting and providing updates on child's condition.

initial_variations: My {victim_relation}, {child_name}, has fallen into a well in our backyard! It's deep and he's crying for help! Please send someone now!
Help! A child fell down a well at {address_location}. We can hear him but we can't reach him. He's scared and cold!
This is an emergency! My {victim_relation} fell into an old well. He's about {depth} feet down and there's water at the bottom!
911, there's a child trapped in a well at {property_name} on {street}. He's been down there for ten minutes and we need rescue immediately!

initial: We are at {address_location} in rural {county}. The well is behind the barn, next to the old oak tree.
Location: {address_location}. The well is in the backyard, covered by some boards that gave way.
Our property is on {street}, about {miles} miles from {nearest_town}. The well is near the garden.
rescue_attempt: Same location: {address_location}. Rescue teams are setting up equipment now.
We're still at {address_location}. The well is narrow so they're using a tripod and harness.
Emergency crews are on site at {address_location}. They are assessing the best approach.
waiting: We're at the well at {address_location}. The child is still down there. We're talking to him to keep him calm.
Still waiting at {address_location}. Firefighters are working on getting him out.
Location unchanged: {address_location}. The child is tired but conscious.

initial: The child is {child_name}, age {age}. He fell in about {minutes} ago. We can see him but he's too deep to reach.
He fell down a well that is about {depth} deep and {width} wide. He's standing on a ledge and there's water up to his waist.
The well is old, possibly {years_old} years old. The sides are crumbling. He's scared but talking.
rescue_attempt: The child is at {depth} down. Rescue has a line down to him. He's secured and they are pulling him up.
He's conscious and responsive. He's cold but no serious injuries. Rescue is progressing slowly due to narrow shaft.
The well is unstable. Rescue is using a spreader bar to avoid collapse. He's doing okay.
waiting: He's getting weaker. He's shivering and says his legs are numb. Please hurry!
The child is crying for his mom. He's scared of the dark. We're shining a light down.
He's calm now, talking to the rescuers. They've reassured him. He says he's okay.

initial: Keep the child warm by dropping a blanket or jacket down. Reassure him that help is coming.
Do not attempt to go down yourself. The well may be unstable. Wait for professional rescuers.
Clear the area around the well. Make sure there are no loose objects that could fall in.
rescue_attempt: Keep talking to the child. Tell him to stay still and not move around too much.
If you have rope, lower it down but don't pull. Let the rescuers handle extraction.
Make sure the well opening is secure and no one else gets near the edge.
waiting: Continue to comfort the child. Ask simple questions to keep him awake and oriented.
If rescue is taking long, consider warming the air being pumped down with a portable heater.
Monitor for signs of hypothermia: shivering, confusion. Report to rescuers.

initial: The area is safe. The child is not in immediate danger of drowning but hypothermia is a risk.
We have called the fire department. They are en route. The child is conscious and we are with him.
Other children are kept away. The well is isolated.
rescue_attempt: Rescue is proceeding safely. No signs of collapse. The child is secure in the harness.
The child is being lifted slowly. Everything is under control.
Crews are working efficiently. The child is about halfway up.
waiting: The child is stable and being monitored. Rescue is expected to be complete in {estimated_time} minutes.
We are all safe. Just waiting for the final extraction.
The situation is controlled. The child will be out soon.

outcomes.feedback: Police dispatched but they are not trained for technical rescue. Fire department should have been sent. Delay in actual rescue.
Police arrived and secured the scene, but fire rescue was needed. Points lost for not dispatching fire immediately.
Fire department rescue team arrived and successfully extracted the child. The child was treated for mild hypothermia and released. Perfect dispatch.
Firefighters used specialized equipment to rescue the child. The child is safe and recovering. Good job.
Medical sent but no rescue capability. Fire department needed for extraction. Medical could not reach the child.
Paramedics on standby, but rescue operations delayed due to lack of equipment. Fire should have been primary.
Animal control dispatched to a child in a well. Completely inappropriate. Child suffered hypothermia while waiting.
Caller confused. No animals involved. Child trapped for hours due to wrong dispatch. Critical failure.
Call dismissed as a prank. The child died from hypothermia and exhaustion. Unforgivable.
You hung up on a frantic parent. Child trapped for hours before another call came in. Serious injury.

title: Chemical Spill at a Factory
description: A worker at an industrial factory calls 911 after a large container of toxic chemical (chlorine) ruptures. Workers are exposed and need evacuation, while hazmat response is critical.

states.initial: Caller reports the spill and provides location and chemical info.
states.evacuating: Caller is leading or participating in evacuation of the facility.
states.contained: Leak is stopped or isolated, and victims are receiving medical attention.

initial_variations: This is {caller_name} at {factory_name} on {street}. We have a major chemical spill! Chlorine gas is filling the building! People are choking!
Emergency at {factory_name}! A tank of {chemical} just ruptured. We need hazmat and medical immediately! Multiple workers are down!
911, I'm at {address_location}. There's a toxic gas leak! I see people collapsing. We need to evacuate but some are trapped!
Please help! Chemical spill at {factory_name}. It's {chemical} and it's spreading fast. Our alarms are going off. Everyone is coughing and eyes burning!

initial: We are at {address_location}. The spill is in building 2, near the loading dock.
Location: {address_location}. This is the main plant of {factory_name}. The leak is in the storage area.
I'm at {factory_name}, {address_location}. The chemical is spreading towards the office area.
evacuating: We are evacuating to the designated meeting point at the front gate of {factory_name}. Address: {address_location}.
Some workers are heading to the east parking lot. Address is still {address_location}.
Moving away from the building. We're at the north side of {factory_name} on {street}.
contained: The leak is now isolated to the containment area. We are at {address_location}. Emergency crews are on scene.
We are all outside the building at {address_location}. Hazmat teams are entering.
The area is secured. Victims are being treated in the triage area at {address_location}.

initial: The chemical is {chemical}. It's a gas leak from a ruptured tank. We see a greenish cloud forming.
It's {chemical} gas. People are experiencing burning eyes, coughing, and difficulty breathing. Some have collapsed.
Approximately {num_affected} people are exposed. The leak is in the main processing hall. We need immediate evacuation.
evacuating: We have evacuated about {num_evacuated} people so far. {num_missing} are unaccounted for. The gas is spreading.
Most workers are out. A few are trapped in the west wing. The gas is thick there.
Evacuation is ongoing. We are using fire alarms to alert everyone. Many are heading to the exits.
contained: The leak is stopped. Hazmat team is monitoring air quality. {num_injured} people are being treated for inhalation.
All personnel are accounted for. {num_hospitalized} are being transported to hospitals. The rest are decontaminated.
The chemical is neutralized. The building is being ventilated. Medical teams are assessing everyone.

initial: Evacuate the building immediately. Move upwind and avoid the vapor cloud. Use wet cloths over mouth and nose if possible.
Do not use elevators. Take stairs. Alert others as you go. If you have a safety shower, use it to decontaminate.
Close doors behind you to contain the spread. Head to the designated assembly point.
evacuating: Keep moving away from the leak. Do not return for personal items. Help anyone who is struggling.
If you see someone collapsed, do not approach if the gas is thick. Notify responders of their location.
Once outside, perform headcount. Check for symptoms: burning eyes, coughing, confusion.
contained: Remain at the assembly point. Do not re-enter until cleared by hazmat. Follow decontamination procedures.
Provide first aid for breathing difficulties. Give oxygen if available. Wait for medical help.
Stay calm. Help the injured. Keep airways clear.

initial: The gas is spreading quickly. It's not safe to stay in the building. Everyone is trying to get out.
We have activated the emergency shutdown. The leak is not yet contained. Evacuation is critical.
The area is hazardous. We need immediate response. Some people are already showing severe symptoms.
evacuating: We are mostly out. The gas has not reached outside yet. We are safe at the assembly point.
The wind is blowing the cloud away from us. We are in a safe zone now.
Evacuation is almost complete. No new exposures. We are monitoring the situation.
contained: The immediate danger is over. Hazmat reports the leak is sealed. Air quality is improving.
Victims are being treated. No new casualties. The area is stable.
All clear for now. The event is under control. Further assessment ongoing.

outcomes.feedback: Police sent but they are not hazmat trained. Fire and hazmat needed. Police can assist with crowd control, but primary response was delayed.
Police arrived and set up perimeter. However, chemical response was delayed. Points lost.
Fire department hazmat team responded, contained the leak, and decontaminated victims. All survivors. Excellent dispatch.
Firefighters stopped the leak and coordinated medical care. Multiple lives saved. Great decision.
Medical dispatched but without hazmat, they could not enter the contaminated area. Fire needed to secure scene first.
Paramedics treated some victims outside, but could not access the trapped ones. Inefficient response.
Animal control sent to a chemical spill. No animals involved. Workers were exposed for hours. Terrible.
Caller confused. No pets or wildlife. Chemical cloud spread to neighborhood. Multiple casualties due to delay.
Call dismissed as false alarm. Explosion occurred minutes later. Multiple fatalities and injuries. Unforgivable.
You ignored the call. The chemical leak caused a chain reaction fire. Several workers died. Massive failure.
